## Research Summary

**Entity:** TRAFIGURA PTE LTD (UEN 199601595D)  
**Singapore Operations HQ of:** Trafigura Group (global commodity trading giant)  
**CEO:** Richard Holtum (CEO since Jan 2025)

### Key Findings by Workstream:

| # | Workstream | Status | Key Observations |
|---|-----------|--------|-----------------|
| 1A | Corporate Registry | ✅ | Live since 07 Mar 1996. Paid-up SGD 6.1M. Principal: Wholesale of fuels. PwC auditor. |
| 2A | Beneficial Ownership | ✅ | 100% employee-owned (~1,400 shareholders). Farringford Foundation (Panama) holds voting control. No outside investors. |
| 3A | Sanctions Screening | ✅ **No SDN hits** | **OFAC-licensed** for Venezuela (Jan 2026). Voluntarily ceased Russia trade (May 2022). |
| 4A | Adverse Media | ⚠️ **HIGH** | $127M DOJ FCPA settlement (Mar 2024). $49M Brazil resolution (Mar 2025). Swiss Angola conviction (Jan 2025, appealed). |
| 5A | Litigation | ⚠️ Active | Won $500M nickel fraud case (Jan 2026). **Active:** Helms-Burton Cuba claim in Delaware. |
| 6A | PEP Check | ✅ **Richard Holtum: NOT PEP** | Former British Army Captain (retired). Private sector exec. Clean background. |
| 7A | Trade Risk | ⚠️ **HIGH** | Operates in sanctioned jurisdictions (Venezuela via OFAC license). Significant corruption history. Post-2019 compliance reforms. |
| 8A | Risk Assessment | ✅ **Score: 4 (REVIEW)** | Major counterparty with sophisticated compliance but significant adverse media and active litigation. |

### Key Individuals:
| Person | Role | PEP? | Sanctions Hit? | Notes |
|--------|------|------|---------------|-------|
| **Richard Holtum** | CEO | ❌ No | ❌ No | British, ex-Army Captain, Sandhurst & St Andrews, Glencore alum, 10yr at Trafigura |

### Risk Summary:
**Score: 4 (REVIEW)** — Enhanced Due Diligence required. Trafigura's corruption history (3 settlements in 2 years) combined with OFAC-licensed Venezuela operations and active Helms-Burton litigation elevate this above standard MONITOR level. Mitigated by strong compliance framework, clean CEO, and demonstrated Russia sanctions compliance.
